title: Shared Libraries and Dynamic Linking
summary: Explains static vs. dynamic (shared) libraries, implicit and explicit dynamic linking, and OS/platform examples (Windows LoadLibrary, Linux dlopen).
content_points: Static library: code included in executable at link time; each program has its own copy.
Shared library (DLL/.so): loaded once into memory; shared by all programs that need it.
Implicit linking: OS loads required libraries on program start; program lists them in import libraries.
Explicit linking: program calls LoadLibrary()/dlopen() at runtime; can load/unload at any time.
Benefits: smaller executables, single on-disk copy, easier updates.
learning_objectives: Explain implicit and explicit dynamic linking
Show Windows and Linux API equivalents for explicit linking
